whatChangedUpstream: GET /prices-history now accepts startTs/endTs windows up to 15 days each; chunking unlocks up to 180 days of fidelity-controlled history per call (previously ~30 days max at fine fidelity, hard cap).
POST /batch-prices-history fetches price history for up to 20 tokens in one request; collapses N sequential calls to ceil(N/20).
CLOB GET /orderbook-history (live-verified, ms timestamps) exposes historical full-book snapshots with limit/offset paging.
Data API GET /v1/market-positions returns per-holder cost basis, realized and unrealized PnL per market.
Data API GET /v1/leaderboard returns trader rankings by profit/volume across day/week/month/all windows.
Gamma /rewards market fields (rewardsDailyRate, spread requirements) enable liquidity-reward screening.
Gamma sports metadata (/sports, /teams, valid market types) enables league/team grounding.
Gamma /events/keyset and /markets/keyset cursor pagination removes the offset ceiling for deep universe scans.
Data API /trades page cap raised to 1000 with offset cap 3000 and eventId/side filters.

alphaCategories.name: deep-price-history
alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: get_price_history (deep window mode, chunked GET /prices-history startTs/endTs), get_batch_price_history (POST /batch-prices-history)
alphaCategories.description: Up to 180 days of fidelity-controlled price history for any outcome token in one call, and multi-token (<=20) history in one batch call.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Free LLMs have zero Polymarket time series; even the Polymarket UI chart widget offers only fixed ranges with no export. Momentum, repricing events, and regime shifts are invisible without this.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Momentum/repricing analysis needs the raw series; training data cannot contain last month's odds path.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Pull the last 60 days of daily Yes prices for [market] and identify the biggest single-week repricing.

alphaCategories.name: pnl-aware-whale-intel
alphaCategories.description: Per-holder cost basis, realized and unrealized PnL for any market -- not just share counts.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Knowing whether the largest holders are in profit or underwater is conviction-grade signal; neither free LLMs nor the Polymarket market page show a PnL-ranked holder table.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Cost-basis-aware whale reads separate diamond-hand conviction from trapped bagholders.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: List top 10 holders of [market] with cost basis and unrealized PnL -- is smart money in profit?

alphaCategories.name: trader-intelligence
alphaCategories.description: Live trader rankings by profit/volume over day/week/month/all, plus consolidated wallet profiles (markets traded, portfolio value, leaderboard standing).
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Who is winning on Polymarket this week is fast-moving, on-chain-derived data absent from any training set.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Copy-trading and counterparty analysis start from knowing who the sharps are right now.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Top 5 traders by profit this week, and the full profile of the #1 wallet.

alphaCategories.name: liquidity-rewards
alphaCategories.upstreamDataSource: find_reward_markets (Gamma /markets rewardsDailyRate fields + CLOB spread enrichment)
alphaCategories.description: Screen for markets currently paying liquidity rewards, with daily USD rates, spread requirements, and current book tightness.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Reward configs change frequently and the upstream UI has no sortable rewards-vs-risk screening table; free LLMs do not know rewards exist per market.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Market-makers earn real yield; reward-per-risk ranking is a paid-only answer.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Which markets pay >= $50/day in rewards with spreads tight enough to quote for a $10k MM budget?

alphaCategories.name: microstructure-history
alphaCategories.description: Historical full-orderbook snapshots sampled across a time range -- how depth and spread evolved, not just the current book.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Historical book states exist nowhere publicly except this endpoint; the UI shows only the live book.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Liquidity thickening/thinning ahead of catalysts is tradable microstructure signal.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: How has book depth for [market] evolved over the past 7 days -- thickening or thinning into the event?

alphaCategories.name: sports-metadata
alphaCategories.description: Live league inventory, team records/images, and per-league valid market types (moneyline, spread, total, props).
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Grounds sports questions in Polymarket's actual live league/team/market-type universe instead of guessing slugs.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Correct sports entity resolution is the gate to every downstream sports-market answer.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: What leagues and market types are live on Polymarket right now for soccer?

alphaCategories.name: deep-universe-scan
alphaCategories.description: Stable cursor paging through the full live event universe with no offset ceiling.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Free LLMs cannot enumerate the live universe at all; offset paging previously silently truncated deep scans.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Census-style questions (how many active events per category) require full traversal.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Page through all live events beyond the first 500 and count active events per category.

alphaCategories.name: pricing-efficiency
alphaCategories.description: Live bid-ask spreads, midpoints, implied probabilities, and fair-value dislocations across the CLOB.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Free LLMs produce stale or hallucinated probabilities; CLOB endpoints expose current tick-level truth.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Tick-level quotes are the ground truth for any live odds question.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Current spread, midpoint, and implied Yes probability for [market]?

alphaCategories.name: liquidity-depth
alphaCategories.description: Orderbook depth, slippage estimates, and fillable size at live prices.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: The only way to answer 'can I get $50k filled at 40c' is walking the live book.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Execution feasibility is invisible to any model without the book.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Slippage for a $50k Yes buy on [market] vs current midpoint?

alphaCategories.name: event-tradability
alphaCategories.description: Ranking outcomes within multi-outcome events by liquidity, spread, volume, and now 24h batch momentum.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Multi-outcome events have dozens of sub-markets with wildly different tradability; batch momentum adds who-is-moving context.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Relative-value across strikes/candidates/teams is a synthesis no free model can ground.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Rank every outcome in [event] by tradability and 24h momentum.

alphaCategories.name: rules-and-resolution
alphaCategories.description: Exact resolution criteria, UMA oracle, end dates, neg-risk semantics, tick/min-size parameters.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Resolution ambiguity is the top reason bettors get burned; literal rules text is required.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Paraphrased rules are dangerous; buyers need the binding text.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Resolution rules, source of truth, and end date for [market]?

alphaCategories.name: screening-and-discovery
alphaCategories.description: Programmatic screens across categories, tags, probability bands, end-date windows, volume/liquidity thresholds.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: Free LLMs cannot enumerate the live Polymarket universe or apply live thresholds.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Actionable shortlists from live data are the core screening product.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Markets with 30-50% implied probability ending within 14 days with >$500k 24h volume.

alphaCategories.name: workflow-synthesis
alphaCategories.description: Multi-step pipelines chaining rules, quotes, whales, history, and depth into a buyer-grade memo.
alphaCategories.whyItBeatsFree: The end deliverable -- a tradable memo with live numbers -- requires chaining live tools.
alphaCategories.whyAlpha: Synthesis across five live data planes is the ceiling product.
alphaCategories.examplePromptShape: Full tradability memo for [market] with rules risk, quotes, whale PnL, 30-day trend, and sizing.

crossToolOpportunities: Fed/macro odds (get_batch_price_history on Fed decision outcomes) x CoinGlass contributor funding/OI: does crypto derivatives positioning agree with prediction-market rate odds?
Polymarket sports moneylines (get_sports_context + compare_event_outcome_quotes) x Odds-API contributor sportsbook lines: venue divergence and middle opportunities.
Polymarket vs Kalshi (in-server polymarket_crossref_kalshi) enriched with 30-day batch history on the Polymarket leg to show which venue repriced first.
upstreamUIGaps: No PnL-ranked holder table per market in the Polymarket UI -- get_market_positions exposes cost basis and unrealized PnL per holder per market.
Chart widget offers fixed ranges with no export; no way to pull 180-day fidelity-controlled series or multi-token comparisons -- deep/batch price history tools do.
No rewards screening table combining daily reward rate, spread requirement, and live book tightness -- find_reward_markets does.
No historical orderbook view at all -- get_orderbook_history is a time machine for the book.
Event browsing is shallow-paged; no census/deep-scan of the full live universe -- keyset cursor paging in get_events removes the ceiling.

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Pull the last 60 days of daily Yes price history for the 'Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?' market and identify the biggest single-week repricing and whether momentum is accelerating.

groundedEntity.title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_price_history daysBack=60 (deep window mode, chunked /prices-history); smoke-verified 60d span with ~300 points
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: 60-day odds path cannot exist in any training set; UI chart has no export

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Compare 30-day price trajectories for every outcome in the 'Fed Decision in July?' event in one shot -- which outcome repriced hardest and when?
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: search_and_get_outcomes -> get_batch_price_history daysBack=30 (POST /batch-prices-history); smoke-verified multi-token series + ranking
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Multi-outcome repricing race in one call; previously N sequential calls

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Netanyahu out by end of 2026?' market, list the top 10 position holders with their cost basis and unrealized PnL -- are the largest Yes holders in profit or underwater?
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_market_positions sortBy=CURRENT conditionId from market lookup; smoke-verified varying avgPrice/cashPnl per holder
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: PnL-ranked holder table exists nowhere in UI or free models

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Who are the top 5 traders on Polymarket by profit this week, and what is the full trading profile of the #1 wallet -- volume, markets traded, and current portfolio value?
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_trader_leaderboard timePeriod=WEEK -> get_wallet_profile on top wallet; smoke-verified live PnL figures ($4.05M weekly #1 at research time)
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: This week's sharps are unknowable to a static model

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Screen Polymarket for markets currently paying at least $50/day in liquidity rewards with tight spread requirements -- which offer the best reward-per-risk for a $10k market-making budget?
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: find_reward_markets minDailyRateUsd=50 enrich=true; smoke-verified rewardsDailyRate + live spread enrichment
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Reward configs are live operational data with no UI screening table

mustWinPrompts.prompt: How has orderbook depth for the 'Will no Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?' market evolved over the past 7 days -- is liquidity thickening or thinning ahead of the next Fed meeting?

groundedEntity.title: Will no Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?

mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_orderbook_history hoursBack=168 (CLOB /orderbook-history, time-uniform samples); smoke-verified snapshots with per-sample depth
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Historical book states are exposed nowhere else

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Using Polymarket's live sports metadata, which leagues are live right now, what market types does each support, and list current moneyline markets for the biggest live soccer competition.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_sports_context (+ league drilldown); smoke-verified league inventory with market types
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Live league/market-type inventory grounds all sports answers

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Page through the live Polymarket event universe beyond the first 500 events using cursor paging and summarize how many active events exist and which categories dominate the long tail.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_events with afterCursor/nextCursor loop (Gamma /events/keyset); smoke-verified stable non-overlapping pages
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Census questions need full traversal; offsets previously truncated

mustWinPrompts.prompt: Build a tradability memo for the 'US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by December 31?' market combining resolution rules risk, current quotes and depth, PnL-aware whale positioning, and the 30-day price trend.

groundedEntity.title: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by December 31?

mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Five live data planes chained into one memo

mustWinPrompts.prompt: For the 'Fed Decision in July?' event: pull 60-day history for the leading outcome, the PnL-ranked top holders, and current book depth, then judge whether smart money agrees with the market-implied probability.
mustWinPrompts.answerabilityNote: get_batch_price_history + get_market_positions + get_orderbook; ceiling synthesis across new July capabilities
mustWinPrompts.whyAlpha: Cross-plane conviction read: price path + holder PnL + live depth
